XSLT/xsl:number/lang.name: lang
XSLT/xsl:number/lang.description: Specifies which language's alphabet should be used in letter-based numbering formats.

info.excerpt: Specifies which language's alphabet should be used in letter-based numbering formats.

XSLT/xsl:sort/lang.name: lang
XSLT/xsl:sort/lang.description: Specifies which language is to be used by the sort.

info.excerpt: Specifies which language is to be used by the sort.

XSLT/xsl:number/letter-value.name: letter-value
XSLT/xsl:number/letter-value.description: Disambiguates between numbering sequences that use letters. Some languages have more than one numbering system that use letters. If both systems begin with the same token, ambiguity can arise. This at

XSLT/xsl:number/level.name: level
XSLT/xsl:number/level.description: Defines how levels of the source tree should be considered in generating sequential numbers. It has three valid values:single,multiple, andany. The default value issingle:singleNumbers sibling nodes s

info.excerpt: Defines how levels of the source tree should be considered in generating sequential numbers. It has three valid values:single,multiple, andany. The default value issingle:singleNumbers sibling nodes s
